complaint_mechanism: EMNRD (waste/capture) with NM Environment Department (NMED) for air emissions
applicable_rule: NMED "Report an Environmental Issue or Incident" online form (https://ents.web.env.nm.gov/public/INCIDENT_HDR_add.php); OCD field offices via https://www.emnrd.nm.gov/ocd/contact-us/
rule_summary: 19.15.27.9 NMAC — Statewide Natural Gas Capture Requirements (part of 19.15.27 NMAC
column6: Venting and Flaring of Natural Gas)
column7: Requires oil and gas operators to capture no less than 98% of produced natural gas by December 31
column8: 2026 (phased from 2022) and prohibits routine venting and flaring except in emergencies or equipment failure. Administered by the Oil Conservation Commission/Division under EMNRD.

regulator_name: Railroad Commission of Texas (RRC) for oil and gas operations with Texas Commission on Environmental Quality (TCEQ) for air emissions
complaint_mechanism: TCEQ environmental complaint form (https://www.tceq.texas.gov/compliance/complaints, 24-hr line 1-888-777-3186); RRC oil and gas complaints via the appropriate district office (https://www.rrc.texas.gov/oil-and-gas/o-g-complaints/)
applicable_rule: 16 TAC 3.32 — Statewide Rule 32 (Gas Well Gas and Casinghead Gas Disposition; venting and flaring limitations)
rule_summary: Governs disposition of gas and limits routine venting and flaring but permits flaring by exception/administrative approval. Texas has no state-level mandate comparable to New Mexico's 98% gas-capture requirement; MethaneSAT data shows Texas Permian operators emit roughly twice the methane per unit of production versus New Mexico.
